summary: Pristine audience recording with flawless sound and a powerhouse set highlighted by an unforgettable Sugaree opener and smoking jam on He's Gone → Truckin' → The Other One.
review: These reviews rave about the glass‑clean capture from Glassberg/MOTB, calling it “pristine” and “full and clear,” with only a few micro‑dropouts that can be fixed. The show itself is described as one of the band’s best – a hummingbird‑fast Jerry, a top‑ten Sugaree, a monster Jack Straw close, and a second‑set jam that lets the Beast loose. Overall the performance and recording are both judged to be exceptional.

ai_rating.rationale: All detailed reviews are five‑star, praising both musical execution (70% weight) and the high‑quality Glassberg/MOTB source (30% weight). Minor dropouts noted do not diminish the overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Pristine, full‑frequency capture typical of Glassberg/MOTB tapes; a handful of micro‑dropouts in Sugaree that some listeners have had to clean up.

standout_songs: He's Gone > Truckin' > The Other One
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds with a hummingbird‑fast Jerry and an epic Sugaree opener that carries through to a soaring Jack Straw. Second set opens strong with China/Rider and then lets loose on the He's Gone → Truckin' → Other One jam, delivering a high‑energy climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as “fingers moving like a hummingbird on crack” and generally sounding great for the month.
band_member_comments.Keys: First‑time appearance of Brent on keyboards; reviewers note he meshed well and added to the music.
